comments.comment: Verified: computeQuality import is commented out at line 17 of main.ts and readSignals() is called inside the async geoPromise.then block. The wiring is straightforward.
Confirmed: _resolutionScale parameter exists at line 5 of scene.ts but is unused. Activating it with `renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2) * resolutionScale)` is correct.

The DPR <= 1 && touch heuristic is a reasonable pre-quality proxy. Note that some modern phones have DPR >= 2, so this will only catch the lowest-end devices. Acceptable tradeoff since antialias cannot be changed post-creation.

Adding quality to LoopDeps is correct but the plan acknowledges no runtime render-loop changes are needed beyond carrying the reference. Consider whether the field is worth adding if unused by the loop itself — it may be useful for future stories.

Verified: createParticleField accepts ParticleFieldConfig { maxParticles?, enableSparkle? }, createWaveField accepts WaveFieldConfig { maxWaves?, enableShimmer? }, and createModeManager accepts ModeEntry[]. The factory pattern in the plan passes already-constructed instances wrapped in factory functions — this works but means factories are called once and return the same object. Functionally fine for mode switching.
Confirmed: waveField.ts respects maxWaves from config with DEFAULT_MAX_WAVES = 20. No code change needed in the file itself.

The plan says to detach the old resize handler and re-attach. main.ts currently calls attachResizeHandler but does not store the returned cleanup function. The implementation must store it: `let detachResize = attachResizeHandler(renderer, camera)` then later `detachResize(); detachResize = attachResizeHandler(renderer, camera, quality.resolutionScale)`.

Existing 8 tests (T-025-01 through T-025-08) are confirmed present and passing. The 3 proposed integration tests (T-025-09 through T-025-11) cover particle count, wave count, and renderer pixel ratio — good coverage for the wiring.

Standard verification step. Note that draw() methods in both particleField and waveField are no-ops (Canvas 2D port TODO), so visual verification will only confirm the placeholder mesh renders — the plan correctly identifies this risk.

risks_assessment: Both identified risks are valid and mitigations are sound. The async quality computation vs sync renderer creation is handled by the two-phase approach (default init then re-apply). The no-op draw methods are a known limitation scoped to a future story.
